stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/ML domain, specifically text generation models.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/LLM domain, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The tags indicate relevance to major frameworks like transformers and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
stories.author: DSemba
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Moebius,' a new image inpainting model.
The model achieves performance comparable to 10B-level models despite having only 0.2B parameters.
This suggests a significant efficiency breakthrough in generative AI/computer vision.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text-generation tasks.
The technical tags indicate the use of standard ML frameworks like transformers and PyTorch.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets and optimizes execution on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Nvidia says its AI data center design runs hotter to use a lot less water
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Nvidia claims its Rubin generation AI data center design significantly reduces both power and water consumption.
The design utilizes fully liquid cooling to achieve these efficiency gains, addressing public concerns about data center impact.
The article notes that while this is a positive step, the technology does not resolve all existing environmental concerns regarding AI data centers.

stories.title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Google's DeepMind is partnering with A24 to develop AI tools for film production.
The collaboration aims to enhance filmmakers' storytelling capabilities using AI technology.
Google is reportedly investing approximately $75 million into A24 as part of the R&D effort.

stories.title: GM installs robots at flagship EV factory after laying off 1,300 workers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: GM is implementing robotics at its flagship EV factory.
This automation comes amidst significant workforce reductions (1,300 layoffs).
The move highlights the growing trend of factory automation and labor concerns in the EV sector.

stories.title: NHTSA investigating alleged Tesla Autopilot crash that killed woman in her home
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: NHTSA is investigating a fatal crash involving a Tesla vehicle using Autopilot.
The incident resulted in the death of a woman in her home.
The report highlights the ongoing public debate regarding the safety and efficacy of Tesla's Autopilot system.

stories.title: Backpropagating Through Simulation: Analytic Policy Gradients for Sample and Learning Efficient Differentiable Continuous Control
stories.author: Yueci Deng
bullet_points: Introduces 'Analytic Policy Gradients' to improve reinforcement learning efficiency.
Leverages differentiable environment dynamics to enable exact gradient computation via backpropagation through simulation.
Addresses the high sample complexity and high-variance issues inherent in traditional black-box RL methods like PPO.

stories.title: Backpropagating Through Simulation: Analytic Policy Gradients for Sample and Learning Efficient Differentiable Continuous Control
stories.author: Yueci Deng
bullet_points: Introduces 'Analytic Policy Gradients' for reinforcement learning when environment dynamics are differentiable.
Addresses the high sample complexity and variance issues of traditional black-box RL methods like PPO.
Leverages backpropagation through simulation to compute exact, end-to-end policy gradients.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It specifically augments Ollama's MLX engine, improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning.
It is designed to support long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM deployment.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing on-device, private AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Valve is working with AMD to bring FSR 4 to the Steam Machine
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Valve is collaborating with AMD to integrate FSR 4 technology into the Steam Machine.
The Steam Machine is noted as having performance comparable to a PlayStation 5.
The current implementation of AMD FSR on the Steam Machine is considered subpar and needs improvement.

stories.title: Valve will finally let you build your own Steam Machine with SteamOS for desktop
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Valve is enabling users to build their own Steam Machines using custom PC parts.
This functionality is being introduced starting with the SteamOS 3.8 release.
The update also includes general improvements to SteamOS compatibility.

stories.title: Report: Kennedy Space Center not ready for era of super heavy rockets
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX plans an aggressive launch cadence of every eight days for Starship from Kennedy Space Center.
A report indicates that the Kennedy Space Center infrastructure may not be prepared for this high-frequency operation.
The story highlights a potential logistical bottleneck between ambitious private space goals and existing government/facility readiness.

stories.title: Polymarket's viral videos showed people winning big, but the bets were fake
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Polymarket's viral success was based on misleading information regarding winnings.
The 'winning' bets were placed on a cloned website, meaning the actual bets would have resulted in losses.
The story highlights issues of deception and trust within the decentralized prediction market space.

stories.title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: AMD has reinstated memory encryption functionality in its consumer CPUs.
The reinstatement followed significant public criticism and user outcry.
Some critics view the move as a strategic tactic to encourage users toward higher-priced AMD chips.

stories.title: A US military exercise in space got underway with barely anyone noticing
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force is focused on drastically accelerating the deployment timeline for new satellites.
The goal is to reduce the time from years down to mere weeks, days, or even hours.
The exercise highlighted the current low public awareness surrounding critical military space operations.

stories.title: FAST: A Framework for Aligned Sampling and Training in Parallel Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Shengbo Eben Li
bullet_points: Addresses sampling efficiency bottlenecks in parallel reinforcement learning for autonomous driving.
Proposes FAST, a synchronous parallel framework to mitigate the 'straggler effect' in RL training.
Aims to improve sample utilization and reduce re-initialization latency in complex driving simulations.

stories.title: Composing Verifiable Conceptual Models via Building Blocks: Towards Design-Time Verification of Agentic AI Workflows
stories.author: Philippe J. Giabbanelli
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in verifying complex Agentic AI workflows during the design phase, rather than just at runtime.
Proposes a design-time verification approach by modeling agentic workflows using conceptual building blocks.
Draws an analogy to traditional modeling and simulation, suggesting a need for coherence checks in multi-agent systems.

stories.title: Balancing Performance and Diversity in GRPO Autoregressive Text-to-Image Post-Training
stories.author: Xueqian Wang
bullet_points: Addresses a key challenge in Text-to-Image (T2I) generation: aligning generated images with human preferences.
Proposes an improvement to GRPO-style online reinforcement learning by treating reference-policy divergence as a variable factor.
Utilizes a unified f-divergence framework (including KL and JS divergence) to enhance policy optimization.

stories.title: Decoupling the Declarative from the Procedural in Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Alexandros Kouris
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical need for transferable skills in generalist robotic agents to avoid intractable data collection.
It critiques the current paradigm of fine-tuning large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on specific demonstrations.
The core research aims to decouple the declarative (what) from the procedural (how) aspects within these models for better generalization.

stories.title: Predicting High-Risk Colorectal Polyps in African Americans Using Pre-Colonoscopy Clinical Features: Machine Learning Model Development and Temporal Validation
stories.author: Hassan Ashktorab
bullet_points: The research focuses on developing a machine learning model to predict high-risk colorectal polyps.
The model utilizes pre-colonoscopy clinical features, aiming for a non-invasive risk stratification approach.
The study specifically targets African American populations to improve clinical decision-making and optimize colonoscopy resource allocation.

stories.title: FAST: A Framework for Aligned Sampling and Training in Parallel Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Shengbo Eben Li
bullet_points: Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous driving: inefficient sampling in parallel reinforcement learning.
Proposes FAST, a synchronous parallel framework designed to overcome the 'straggler effect' in RL training.
Aims to improve sample utilization and reduce latency associated with environment re-initialization in complex driving simulations.

stories.title: Compressing Observation History into Agent Memory: Distilling Transformers into Recurrent Transformers
stories.author: Gianluca Monaci
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of Transformers when processing long sequential data.
Proposes 'Recurrent Transformers' to maintain fixed-size memory, suitable for long-horizon applications like robotics.
Aims to improve upon existing Recurrent Transformer performance while mitigating the memory constraints of standard Transformers.

stories.title: Memory Is No Longer a Bottleneck: Memory-Efficient Graph Filtering for Scalable Collaborative Filtering
stories.author: Won-Yong Shin
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) in Collaborative Filtering (CF).
It proposes using training-free Graph Filtering (GF)-based CF methods as a more computationally efficient alternative.
Polynomial GF approaches are highlighted for improving accuracy by effectively smoothing graph signals.

stories.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Temporal Graph Learning with Digital Twin--Guided Adaptive Deception for Cyber-Resilient IoMT
stories.author: Waqas Ali
bullet_points: Addresses critical cybersecurity vulnerabilities in IoMT/IoT environments, particularly in healthcare.
Proposes a novel privacy-preserving defense architecture called Federated TGCN-A2C.
Integrates Federated Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks with adaptive deception techniques for resilience.

stories.title: Balancing Performance and Diversity in GRPO Autoregressive Text-to-Image Post-Training
stories.author: Xueqian Wang
bullet_points: Addresses a challenge in Text-to-Image (T2I) generation: aligning generated images with human preferences.
Proposes an improvement to GRPO-style reinforcement learning by treating reference-policy divergence as a variable factor.
Utilizes a unified f-divergence framework (including KL and JS divergences) to optimize the policy.

stories.title: Dissecting Agentic RAG: A Component Ablation for Multi-Hop QA with a Local 7B Model
stories.author: Sheroz Shaikh
bullet_points: The paper investigates the components of Agentic RAG systems, which use iterative reasoning and query decomposition for complex multi-hop QA.
It specifically analyzes the effectiveness of complex agentic features (like adaptive retrieval routing) when implemented using resource-constrained local 7B language models.
The research aims to clarify which parts of the complex agentic design actually contribute to performance improvements, challenging assumptions about added complexity.

stories.title: MedHal-Loc: Are "Explainable-by-Architecture" Medical Hallucination Detectors Faithful Localizers? A Localization Benchmark
stories.author: Fengdan Chen
bullet_points: Introduces MedHal-Loc, a new benchmark for detecting medical hallucinations in clinical text.
Focuses on 'localization faithfulness,' assessing if error detectors accurately pinpoint the specific faulty span in text.
Challenges existing architectures (like those based on KG triple decomposition) whose localization abilities are often assumed rather than empirically measured.

stories.title: Towards Pedagogically Aligned LLM Tutors for Math Mistake Remediation
stories.author: Ekaterina Kochmar
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLMs in education: failing to adhere to effective pedagogical strategies.
A novel two-stage alignment pipeline (SFT + DPO) is proposed for improving LLM performance in math mistake remediation.
The methodology involves creating a specialized dataset by combining existing tutoring corpora with synthetically generated preference pairs.

stories.title: Evaluation of Small Language Models for Arabic Language Processing
stories.author: Rafik Madani
bullet_points: The research evaluates twelve Small Language Models (SLMs) specifically for Arabic NLP tasks.
A new benchmark comprising 240 Arabic test items across eight domains and ten language skills was introduced.
Evaluation was conducted in a zero-shot setting using an LLM-as-a-judge framework for assessment.

stories.title: Precision Recall Controllable Radiology Report Generation via Hybrid Natural Language and Clinical Reward Learning
stories.author: Dufan Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in automated radiology report generation (RRG): existing methods prioritize language fluency over clinical accuracy.
The proposed solution introduces a hybrid approach combining Natural Language Generation (NLG) with Clinical Reward Learning.
The goal is to enable better control over clinically critical metrics like precision and recall in the generated reports.

stories.title: CAT-Translate: Building Compact Open-Source Models for Japanese-English Translation
stories.author: Yuu Jinnai
bullet_points: Introduces CAT-Translate, a family of compact, open-source language models for Japanese-English translation.
Addresses the practical trade-off between using large, general multilingual models versus specialized, smaller models.
Offers various model sizes (0.8B to 7B parameters) to cater to different resource constraints.

stories.title: Sexualised synthetic personas encode and amplify gendered power asymmetries through voice
stories.author: Eva Szekely
bullet_points: The research analyzes how sexualized, AI-generated voices from a commercial platform encode and amplify gendered power imbalances.
Using a Feminist HCI perspective, the study investigates how commercial voice AI reproduces specific gender performances.
The findings highlight the persistence of toxic masculinity and heteronormativity within current AI systems, despite potential for empowerment.

stories.title: The AI Security Gap: Why your autonomous agents are completely unprotected
stories.author: magopredator
bullet_points: Focuses on the critical security vulnerabilities of autonomous AI agents.
Highlights the current lack of adequate protection mechanisms for these agents.
Addresses a growing and important security gap in modern AI development.

stories.title: What Happens When We Stop Asking
stories.author: eayurt
bullet_points: The article posits that AI is causing a significant decline in questions asked on platforms like Stack Overflow.
Stack Overflow's monthly question volume is projected to drop drastically from 200k in 2014 to only 3k in 2026.
The author expresses a concern that extends beyond the decline of Q&A platforms due to AI's impact.
